file.content:
**Skill guidance** (the important part, since Claude writes these). The skill should say something like:

> A `problem` is a **one-paragraph framing of the real-world pain the project addresses**. It is not a description of the solution. Good problem statements:
> - Name the person or situation feeling the pain
> - Describe the current workaround or lack thereof
> - Explain why the pain is worth solving right now, not later
> - Avoid naming the implementation ("we'll use Raspberry Pis" belongs in `description`, not `problem`)
>
> Template: *"When [situation], [person] needs [outcome] because [reason/cost of not having it]. Today they [current hacky workaround or nothing]."*
>
> Examples:
> - **Good**: "When I'm debugging a flaky service on the VM, I don't have a fast way to correlate logs across the three systemd units. Today I `journalctl -u ...` in three panes and eyeball timestamps, which is slow and error-prone."
> - **Bad**: "Build a log aggregator with OpenSearch and Grafana." (That's the solution.)
> - **Bad**: "Logs are annoying." (No situation, no person, no cost.)
>
> Before calling `create_project`, draft the problem statement, read it back to the user, and only call the tool once the user approves.

A couple of questions on this:
1. **Minimum length** — should we enforce one? I'd start with "trimmed non-empty" only and rely on the skill + your review. Easy to add later.
2. **Show the problem on the canvas overview?** I proposed hover-tooltip only. Or: at max zoom the full problem renders under the project label?
3. **User approval before create** — the skill sketch above says Claude reads the problem back and waits for OK. Sound right, or do you want it silent?

## Change 2 — proper MCP OAuth 2.1 flow

Yes, this is the right call. I dug into the base Caddyfile and there's a nicer path than I first thought — we can do real OAuth 2.1 **without duplicating Google OAuth in the Go server**.

### The base Caddyfile reality

```
@protected {
    not path /oauth2/* /logout
    not path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|svg|png)$
}
forward_auth @protected localhost:3000 { uri /api/auth/verify ... }
```

Everything except `/oauth2/*`, `/logout`, and favicon goes through `alive-server`'s auth. If you're not authed, alive-server redirects you to Google OAuth, sets a session cookie, sends you back to the original URL.

That existing Google OAuth is **exactly** what we need for the browser-facing part of MCP OAuth. We shouldn't re-implement it in petboard.

### Hybrid architecture

Petboard implements only the MCP-specific OAuth 2.1 endpoints. The Google OAuth part is inherited from Caddy+alive-server.

Routes and their auth posture:

| path                                            | Caddy auth | who handles auth                                        |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `/petboard/` (UI)                               | yes        | Caddy → alive-server → Google (existing)                |
| `/petboard/api/*`                               | yes        | same                                                    |
| `/petboard/events` (SSE)                        | yes        | same                                                    |
| `/petboard/oauth/authorize`                     | **yes**    | same — we *want* Caddy auth here (reuse browser session) |
| `/petboard/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` | no       | public metadata                                         |
| `/petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource`   | no       | public metadata                                         |
| `/petboard/oauth/register`                      | no         | dynamic client registration (RFC 7591)                  |
| `/petboard/oauth/token`                         | no         | petboard validates PKCE + auth code                     |
| `/petboard/mcp`                                 | no         | petboard validates Bearer access token                  |

The **one base Caddyfile change** this needs is to extend the `@protected` matcher so those public paths bypass forward_auth:

```caddyfile
@protected {
    not path /oauth2/* /logout
    not path /petboard/.well-known/* /petboard/oauth/register /petboard/oauth/token /petboard/mcp
    not path_regexp ^/.*favicon\.(ico|svg|png)$
}
```

I know `base-setup/CLAUDE.md` says *"don't add service routes to the base Caddyfile"*, but this isn't a route — it's an auth exemption that a new service genuinely needs. I'll flag it as a deliberate exception and document the rationale in the petboard install script.

### End-to-end flow

1. Claude Code connects to `https://attlas.uk/petboard/mcp` → 401 with `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata="/petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource"`
2. Claude fetches the well-known docs → learns about petboard's auth endpoints
3. Claude POSTs `/petboard/oauth/register` → gets a `client_id`
4. Claude builds an `/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=...&code_challenge=...&redirect_uri=http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback&...` URL and opens your default browser
5. Caddy sees no session on `/petboard/oauth/authorize` → redirects to Google OAuth → Google → alive-server sets session → back to `/petboard/oauth/authorize?...` with session cookie
6. Petboard's `/authorize` handler generates an auth code, stores `(code, code_challenge, client_id)` in SQLite, 302's to `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/callback?code=...`
7. Claude Code's local listener captures the code, POSTs `/petboard/oauth/token` with code + PKCE verifier → petboard validates, mints an opaque access token, stores it, returns it
8. Claude Code uses `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on all subsequent `/petboard/mcp` calls. Petboard validates the token against SQLite on each request.

**No `petboard auth login` CLI**. No cookie copying. Claude Code drives the whole flow — exactly what you wanted. The only thing you do is click through a Google login screen once (or not even that, if Caddy's session is still fresh).

### What this adds to the implementation

- A small `oauth/` package in the Go server — ~250-400 lines total covering: DCR endpoint, authorize endpoint, token endpoint, PKCE validation, well-known metadata, bearer middleware.
- New tables in SQLite: `oauth_clients`, `oauth_auth_codes`, `oauth_access_tokens`.
- No external library — hand-rolled minimal OAuth 2.1. Fosite is overkill for a single-user service and drags in a pile of dependencies.
- One Caddyfile change in `base-setup/Caddyfile` (documented as a deliberate exception).

### Questions before I write the plan

1. **Caddyfile change** — OK with me editing `base-setup/Caddyfile` to add the petboard path exemptions? It's the one place I can't avoid touching outside of `services/petboard/`. Alternative is implementing Google OAuth a second time inside petboard, which is way more code.
2. **Token lifetime** — access tokens expire? I'd default to **no expiry** for a single-user personal tool, revocable via a `DELETE /api/oauth/tokens/:id` admin endpoint. Standard is 1 hour + refresh tokens, but that's extra complexity for you specifically.
3. **Problem field — answers to the three sub-questions above** (min length, canvas visibility, user-approval-before-create)?

Once those three land I'll write the updated plan doc.

Change 2 needs more thought, because the constraint you're pushing on is real and there's no escape without touching *something* outside petboard. Let me be honest about why, then lay out your options.

## Why petboard alone can't handle this

Caddy is the TLS terminator and the first thing every request hits. The base Caddyfile says: "for everything except `/oauth2/*`, `/logout`, and favicons, do a `forward_auth` subrequest to alive-server." Alive-server checks the session cookie and, if absent, returns a 302 redirect to Google OAuth.

```
[client] → Caddy → forward_auth → alive-server (/api/auth/verify)
                                  ↓
                        200 OK            302 to Google
                          ↓                     ↓
                        petboard         client sees redirect
```

If Claude Code hits `/petboard/mcp` with a Bearer token, the request is checked by **alive-server before petboard ever sees it**. Alive-server has no session cookie, returns 302 to Google, Claude gets a redirect instead of a 401. MCP auth breaks.

Petboard runs *after* this gate. There is literally no code we can write inside the petboard binary that will stop alive-server from being asked first. The only way to make petboard see those requests is to tell the layer in front of it "skip auth for these paths."

That layer is either Caddy or alive-server. Not petboard.

## Your real options

Let me check alive-server briefly so I can give you accurate tradeoffs.

## The move

Caddy's `forward_auth` automatically sends `X-Forwarded-Uri` with the original request path to alive-server on every auth check. Alive-server can read that header and decide whether the path needs auth. **No Caddyfile change, ever.**

Two variants:

### V1 — hardcoded petboard paths in alive-server (smallest)

~15 lines added to `handleAuthVerify`:

```go
var publicPaths = []string{
    "/petboard/mcp",
    "/petboard/.well-known/",
    "/petboard/oauth/register",
    "/petboard/oauth/token",
}

func handleAuthVerify(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    origURI := r.Header.Get("X-Forwarded-Uri")
    for _, p := range publicPaths {
        if strings.HasPrefix(origURI, p) {
            w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
            return
        }
    }
    if isAuthenticated(r) {
        w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
    } else {
        http.Redirect(w, r, "/oauth2/login", http.StatusFound)
    }
}
```

Simple. Couples alive-server to petboard, but realistically there's only ever going to be one MCP-serving service and the change is tiny.

### V2 — generic public-path registry (cleaner, slightly more code)

Alive-server reads `/etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf` on each verify (with a small in-memory cache + file watcher). Each line is a path prefix. Any service that needs unauthenticated paths drops its own file during install:

```
# /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/petboard.conf
/petboard/mcp
/petboard/.well-known/
/petboard/oauth/register
/petboard/oauth/token
```

Alive-server becomes a proper auth gate with a public-path registry. Future services (MCP or otherwise) register themselves cleanly. About ~40 lines in alive-server, no coupling to petboard, good for attlas as a whole.

**My recommendation: V2**. Same effect for your current use case, and if attlas ever grows another public-path-needing service, it's already solved.

## How `/authorize` still works

The authorize endpoint stays protected. Flow:

1. Claude Code opens browser to `/petboard/oauth/authorize?...`
2. Caddy → alive-server verify → path is NOT in public list → check session cookie
3. No session → 302 to `/oauth2/login` → Google → `/oauth2/callback` → session cookie set → redirect back to `/petboard/oauth/authorize?...`
4. Re-request now passes alive-server verify → reaches petboard's authorize handler
5. Petboard generates auth code, 302s to Claude's local callback
6. Claude exchanges code at `/petboard/oauth/token` (public path → straight to petboard), gets access token
7. Claude calls `/petboard/mcp` with Bearer token (public path → straight to petboard → petboard validates token)

So the user-session part reuses Caddy+alive-server's existing Google OAuth (no duplication), and only the MCP-specific OAuth 2.1 endpoints run through petboard's own code. Claude Code drives the whole flow — no `petboard auth login`, no cookie copying.

## The other locked-in answers

- **Problem field**: no min length ✓, tooltip + full-zoom rendering under the project label ✓, Claude reads back and waits for user approval before `create_project` ✓.
- **Token lifetime**: 30 days, sliding window on use. Refresh tokens add complexity I'd skip; when a token expires, Claude Code just runs the OAuth flow again (one browser click if the Caddy session is still fresh, two if you're fully logged out). Stored in SQLite with `(token_hash, created_at, last_used_at, expires_at)`.
- **User approval before create**: confirmed ✓ (I'm reading the cut-off "3." as a confirmation — let me know if it was meant to be something else).

## One question to lock in before the plan doc

**V1 or V2** for the alive-server change? V1 is 15 lines + zero generality. V2 is ~40 lines + a reusable registry. Both keep base Caddyfile untouched and keep MCP auth fully in Go-land as you asked.

aebe49d5-d184-4738-b33f-a96145324d7d [assistant] ## Status checkpoint

We're at a natural deployable slice. Tasks 1–5 done:

| task | what landed |
|------|------|
| 1 | Probed Caddy; uncovered that alive-server didn't preserve return URLs across OAuth |
| 2 | alive-server: public-path registry via `/etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf` + fsnotify-less SIGHUP reload + return-URL preservation across the Google OAuth round-trip |
| 3 | petboard tree scaffolded (go module, vite+react+ts+tailwind+konva+react-query+react-router setup) |
| 4 | Backend v1: SQLite with WAL + FK + busy_timeout, embedded migrations, seed-on-empty, full CRUD service layer, REST handlers with sentinel-error mapping, graceful shutdown, SPA-aware static file server with `/petboard` prefix stripping |
| 5 | `install-petboard.sh` + `uninstall-petboard.sh`: `petboard-svc` user, `/var/lib/petboard`, build-as-agnostic-user with `go mod tidy`, systemd unit, public-path file, `systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP alive-server`, Caddy snippet |

**What's deployable right now:** on the VM, running `sudo bash attlas/services/install-petboard.sh` will build the frontend scaffold + Go binary, start the service, register the public paths, and signal alive-server to reload. The React page will show "petboard / scaffold — the universe will live here" at `/petboard/`, and `GET /petboard/api/projects` (through a browser session cookie) should return the seed data with petboard itself as the first project.

**The decision point**: do you want to deploy this slice now and verify it end-to-end on the VM, or keep me building locally through task #6 (real frontend list view) → #7 (detail page) → #8 (canvas v1) before we deploy?

I'd personally lean toward **deploy now** — it's cheap, it surfaces any packaging/auth/path-stripping surprises early, and "see petboard list itself through the real REST API" is a cool first milestone. But it requires either you running the install script on the VM, or me doing it via your `/terminal` session (which I don't have direct access to from here).

If you want to deploy: run `sudo bash attlas/services/install-petboard.sh` on the VM (note: alive-server needs to be rebuilt and restarted first so the public-path registry + return-URL changes are live — `cd base-setup/alive-server && go build && sudo systemctl restart alive-server`). Then paste any errors or the curl output.

If you want me to keep going: say the word and I'll continue into task #6.

content.text: I'm already on the VM. Good. Let me create the remaining 13 tasks in parallel.

input.subject: Implement alive-server public-path registry (V2) and return-URL preservation
input.description: Two related changes to base-setup/alive-server/main.go: (A) Generic public-path registry that reads /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf into an in-memory prefix list at startup and on SIGHUP. handleAuthVerify consults the registry first using X-Forwarded-Uri and short-circuits to 200 OK for any registered prefix. (B) Return-URL preservation across the Google OAuth round-trip: handleAuthVerify passes the original URI as return_to=, the state store carries it through to handleOAuth2Callback, and the callback redirects to the captured URL (validated via isSafeRelativePath). All changes already in commit 3d347fa on origin/main.

content.content: Task #2 created successfully: Implement alive-server public-path registry (V2) and return-URL preservation

Both go build and npm run build must succeed on the empty scaffold.

input.subject: Build petboard backend v1 (CRUD + seed loader)
input.description: SQLite migrations (0001_init.sql with projects+features+effort_logs, mandatory problem field). Projects CRUD with validation (non-empty trimmed problem). Features CRUD with auto-timestamp on status transitions. Effort logs append-only with aggregate update. Bootstrap seed runs on empty DB and plants petboard as the first project. List endpoint returns aggregates (feature_counts, total_minutes). Verified locally with curl on http://127.0.0.1:7690/petboard/...

input.subject: Install script, systemd unit, Caddy snippet
input.description: Complete install-petboard.sh: create petboard-svc nologin user, provision /var/lib/petboard, build web + server as agnostic-user with explicit PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:..., install binary, write systemd unit, install /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/petboard.conf, deploy petboard.caddy, systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP alive-server. Write uninstall-petboard.sh that keeps /var/lib/petboard. PATH fix landed after first VM deploy attempt failed with "go: command not found" because sudo -u runs a non-login shell.

input.subject: Frontend scaffold (fetch /api/projects, render list)
input.description: Add react-router with basename="/petboard" and two routes: / → Universe, /p/:slug → ProjectDetail. Add QueryClientProvider around the app. Build a tiny api/client.ts — a thin wrapper over fetch that prepends /petboard/api and throws on non-2xx responses. In Universe.tsx fetch /api/projects via useQuery and render a plain unordered list: name, priority pill, progress ring ("7/12"), total hours. Ugly is fine, no canvas yet. This is the smoke test that proves the frontend ↔ backend round-trips through the real auth gate on the VM.

content.content: Task #6 created successfully: Frontend scaffold (fetch /api/projects, render list)

input.subject: Build project detail page
input.description: Route /petboard/p/:slug. Header with name + priority + color + created date + total effort. Editable problem block as pull-quote (click to edit, blur to save, PATCH to API). Four-column board: Backlog / In Progress / Done / Dropped. Feature cards with title, dates, status chip. "Add feature" and "Log effort" buttons. Sparkline of effort log. Back-to-universe link.

content.content: Task #7 created successfully: Build project detail page

input.subject: Canvas v1: react-konva with threads, orbs, pan/zoom, semantic zoom
input.description: Universe page at /petboard/. react-konva Stage with pan (drag empty space) and wheel zoom (centered on cursor). Render project threads as colored glowing lines from created_at to now. Feature orbs at created_at + lines to completed_at where applicable. Status-based orb appearance (open ring backlog, solid done, pulsing in_progress, faint dropped). Vertical "now" line. Semantic zoom: zoomed-out shows just threads; mid zoom shows project labels + progress; zoomed-in shows feature cards.

content.content: Task #8 created successfully: Canvas v1: react-konva with threads, orbs, pan/zoom, semantic zoom

input.subject: Canvas v2: drag-to-reposition, filters, time window, polish
input.description: Drag project label vertically to update canvas_y (PATCH on drop). Priority filter chips and status filter chips in top-left toolbar. Time-window slider at the top of the canvas that fades features outside the window to near-zero opacity (so completed features can stay "forever" but hide on demand). Top-right toolbar with zoom buttons + fit-all. Click a feature orb → side drawer with details. Click project label → navigate to /p/:slug.

input.subject: Implement petboard OAuth 2.1 endpoints
input.description: Add migration 0002_oauth.sql (oauth_clients, oauth_auth_codes, oauth_access_tokens — all tokens as SHA-256 hashes). Implement /petboard/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server and /petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource metadata endpoints. POST /petboard/oauth/register (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration). GET /petboard/oauth/authorize (Caddy-authed, mints auth code with PKCE challenge). POST /petboard/oauth/token (PKCE verifier check, mints 30-day access token). Bearer middleware for /petboard/mcp.

All tools delegate to the service package — same code path the REST API uses.

content.content:
diff --git a/base-setup/alive-server/main.go b/base-setup/alive-server/main.go
index ac745f5..2283a35 100644
--- a/base-setup/alive-server/main.go
+++ b/base-setup/alive-server/main.go
@@ -879,6 +879,150 @@ func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
 	sendJSON(w, detail)
 }
 
+// --- Terminal detail (tmux-backed ttyd sessions) ---
+//
+// All shells served by /terminal/ are wrapped in tmux via
+// services/ttyd-tmux.sh. The tmux server uses a named socket
+// (-L attlas) under /tmp/tmux-<uid>/attlas, owned by the SERVICE_USER
+// the ttyd systemd unit runs as (agnostic-user by default). To read /
+// write that socket the dashboard (running as alive-svc) shells out
+// via `sudo -n -u agnostic-user tmux -L attlas …`, authorized by
+// /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-tmux installed by install-terminal.sh.
+
+const (
+	terminalSocket      = "attlas"
+	terminalSessionUser = "agnostic-user"
+)
+
+// Session names are echoed back from tmux and also passed to the
+// client for the attach URL (/terminal/?arg=<name>). Limit them to a
+// safe character class so neither the shell wrapper nor a malformed
+// HTML attribute can be subverted.
+var terminalSessionRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,32}$`)
+
+type TerminalSession struct {
+	Name      string `json:"name"`
+	Windows   int    `json:"windows"`
+	CreatedAt string `json:"created_at"`      // ISO
+	Created   string `json:"created_rel"`     // "3 h ago"
+	Attached  bool   `json:"attached"`
+	Activity  string `json:"activity_rel"`    // last activity, relative
+}
+
+type TerminalDetail struct {
+	Running  bool              `json:"running"`  // ttyd systemd unit
+	Uptime   string            `json:"uptime"`   // ttyd unit uptime
+	Sessions []TerminalSession `json:"sessions"`
+	Error    string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// tmuxCmd builds an `sudo -n -u agnostic-user tmux -L attlas …` call.
+func tmuxCmd(ctx context.Context, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
+	full := append([]string{"-n", "-u", terminalSessionUser, "/usr/bin/tmux", "-L", terminalSocket}, args...)
+	return exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sudo", full...)
+}
+
+// listTmuxSessions queries tmux for all sessions on the shared
+// socket. Returns an empty slice (not an error) when tmux has no
+// server running, which is the common case before anyone has opened
+// /terminal/ for the first time since boot.
+func listTmuxSessions() ([]TerminalSession, error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+

+	out, err := tmuxCmd(ctx, "list-sessions", "-F", fmtStr).CombinedOutput()
+	if err != nil {
+		// tmux exits 1 with "no server running on ..." when idle.
+		if strings.Contains(string(out), "no server running") {
+			return []TerminalSession{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", err, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)))
+	}
+
+	var sessions []TerminalSession
+	for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
+		if line == "" {
+			continue

+		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
+		if len(parts) < 5 {

diff --git a/base-setup/alive-server/main.go b/base-setup/alive-server/main.go
index ac745f5..2283a35 100644
--- a/base-setup/alive-server/main.go
+++ b/base-setup/alive-server/main.go
@@ -879,6 +879,150 @@ func handleOpenclawDetail(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
 	sendJSON(w, detail)
 }
 
+// --- Terminal detail (tmux-backed ttyd sessions) ---
+//
+// All shells served by /terminal/ are wrapped in tmux via
+// services/ttyd-tmux.sh. The tmux server uses a named socket
+// (-L attlas) under /tmp/tmux-<uid>/attlas, owned by the SERVICE_USER
+// the ttyd systemd unit runs as (agnostic-user by default). To read /
+// write that socket the dashboard (running as alive-svc) shells out
+// via `sudo -n -u agnostic-user tmux -L attlas …`, authorized by
+// /etc/sudoers.d/alive-svc-tmux installed by install-terminal.sh.
+
+const (
+	terminalSocket      = "attlas"
+	terminalSessionUser = "agnostic-user"
+)
+
+// Session names are echoed back from tmux and also passed to the
+// client for the attach URL (/terminal/?arg=<name>). Limit them to a
+// safe character class so neither the shell wrapper nor a malformed
+// HTML attribute can be subverted.
+var terminalSessionRE = regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,32}$`)
+
+type TerminalSession struct {
+	Name      string `json:"name"`
+	Windows   int    `json:"windows"`
+	CreatedAt string `json:"created_at"`      // ISO
+	Created   string `json:"created_rel"`     // "3 h ago"
+	Attached  bool   `json:"attached"`
+	Activity  string `json:"activity_rel"`    // last activity, relative
+}
+
+type TerminalDetail struct {
+	Running  bool              `json:"running"`  // ttyd systemd unit
+	Uptime   string            `json:"uptime"`   // ttyd unit uptime
+	Sessions []TerminalSession `json:"sessions"`
+	Error    string            `json:"error,omitempty"`
+}
+
+// tmuxCmd builds an `sudo -n -u agnostic-user tmux -L attlas …` call.
+func tmuxCmd(ctx context.Context, args ...string) *exec.Cmd {
+	full := append([]string{"-n", "-u", terminalSessionUser, "/usr/bin/tmux", "-L", terminalSocket}, args...)
+	return exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sudo", full...)
+}
+
+// listTmuxSessions queries tmux for all sessions on the shared
+// socket. Returns an empty slice (not an error) when tmux has no
+// server running, which is the common case before anyone has opened
+// /terminal/ for the first time since boot.
+func listTmuxSessions() ([]TerminalSession, error) {
+	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Second)
+	defer cancel()
+
+	// Format: name|windows|created(unix)|attached(0/1)|last_activity(unix)
+	fmtStr := "#{session_name}|#{session_windows}|#{session_created}|#{session_attached}|#{session_activity}"
+	out, err := tmuxCmd(ctx, "list-sessions", "-F", fmtStr).CombinedOutput()
+	if err != nil {
+		// tmux exits 1 with "no server running on ..." when idle.
+		if strings.Contains(string(out), "no server running") {
+			return []TerminalSession{}, nil
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", err, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)))
+	}
+
+	var sessions []TerminalSession
+	for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), "\n") {
+		if line == "" {
+			continue
+		}
+		parts := strings.Split(line, "|")
+		if len(parts) < 5 {
+			continue
+		}
+		windows, _ := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])

content.subject: Probe Caddy forward_auth for X-Forwarded-Uri
content.description: Verify that Caddy's forward_auth directive automatically sets the X-Forwarded-Uri header on the subrequest to alive-server's /api/auth/verify, so alive-server can read the original request URI to make public-path decisions. Also discovered that alive-server did not preserve return URLs across the Google OAuth round-trip — fixed in task #2.

content.subject: SSE live updates from server to canvas
content.description: Server: emit SSE events on every mutation (project.created, project.updated, feature.created, feature.status_changed, effort.logged, etc.) on /petboard/api/events. Client: subscribe via EventSource, invalidate react-query caches on matching events so the canvas animates when Claude or the UI mutates state.

content.subject: Implement petboard OAuth 2.1 endpoints
content.description: Add migration 0002_oauth.sql (oauth_clients, oauth_auth_codes, oauth_access_tokens — all tokens as SHA-256 hashes). Implement /petboard/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server and /petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource metadata endpoints. POST /petboard/oauth/register (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration). GET /petboard/oauth/authorize (Caddy-authed, mints auth code with PKCE challenge). POST /petboard/oauth/token (PKCE verifier check, mints 30-day access token). Bearer middleware for /petboard/mcp.

content.subject: Implement MCP endpoint and tools
content.description: Streamable HTTP MCP transport at /petboard/mcp behind Bearer middleware. Expose tools: list_projects, get_project, create_project (problem required), update_project, add_feature, set_feature_status, update_feature, log_effort, search. All tools delegate to the service package — same code path the REST API uses.

content.subject: Write Claude Code skill and connect end-to-end
content.description: Create dotfiels/claude/skills/petboard.md with the trigger conditions, problem-statement writing guidance (with good/bad examples and the "when/who/because/today" template), session start/end effort logging policy, priority bumping policy, naming conventions for features. End-to-end test: start fresh Claude session, talk about a new pet project, verify it gets created via MCP and shows up on the canvas.

content.subject: Polish: errors, empty states, keyboard shortcuts
content.description: Consistent error rendering (toast + inline), empty states for the universe view and the project board, keyboard shortcuts (j/k to cycle project focus, space to fit-all, escape to close drawer), loading skeletons, dark-mode polish.

content.subject: Implement alive-server public-path registry (V2) and return-URL preservation
content.description: Two related changes to base-setup/alive-server/main.go: (A) Generic public-path registry that reads /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf into an in-memory prefix list at startup and on SIGHUP. handleAuthVerify consults the registry first using X-Forwarded-Uri and short-circuits to 200 OK for any registered prefix. (B) Return-URL preservation across the Google OAuth round-trip: handleAuthVerify passes the original URI as return_to=, the state store carries it through to handleOAuth2Callback, and the callback redirects to the captured URL (validated via isSafeRelativePath). All changes already in commit 3d347fa on origin/main.

content.subject: Scaffold petboard directory tree
content.description: Create the services/petboard/ layout per PLAN.md: server/ (go module, cmd, db, api, oauth, mcp, service, static), web/ (Vite + React + TS + Tailwind project), install-petboard.sh stub, uninstall-petboard.sh stub, petboard.caddy, README.md. Both go build and npm run build must succeed on the empty scaffold.

content.subject: Build petboard backend v1 (CRUD + seed loader)
content.description: SQLite migrations (0001_init.sql with projects+features+effort_logs, mandatory problem field). Projects CRUD with validation (non-empty trimmed problem). Features CRUD with auto-timestamp on status transitions. Effort logs append-only with aggregate update. Bootstrap seed runs on empty DB and plants petboard as the first project. List endpoint returns aggregates (feature_counts, total_minutes). Verified locally with curl on http://127.0.0.1:7690/petboard/...

content.subject: Install script, systemd unit, Caddy snippet
content.description: Complete install-petboard.sh: create petboard-svc nologin user, provision /var/lib/petboard, build web + server as agnostic-user with explicit PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:..., install binary, write systemd unit, install /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/petboard.conf, deploy petboard.caddy, systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP alive-server. Write uninstall-petboard.sh that keeps /var/lib/petboard. PATH fix landed after first VM deploy attempt failed with "go: command not found" because sudo -u runs a non-login shell.

content.subject: Frontend scaffold (fetch /api/projects, render list)
content.description: Add react-router with basename="/petboard" and two routes: / → Universe, /p/:slug → ProjectDetail. Add QueryClientProvider around the app. Build a tiny api/client.ts — a thin wrapper over fetch that prepends /petboard/api and throws on non-2xx responses. In Universe.tsx fetch /api/projects via useQuery and render a plain unordered list: name, priority pill, progress ring ("7/12"), total hours. Ugly is fine, no canvas yet. This is the smoke test that proves the frontend ↔ backend round-trips through the real auth gate on the VM.

content.subject: Build project detail page
content.description: Route /petboard/p/:slug. Header with name + priority + color + created date + total effort. Editable problem block as pull-quote (click to edit, blur to save, PATCH to API). Four-column board: Backlog / In Progress / Done / Dropped. Feature cards with title, dates, status chip. "Add feature" and "Log effort" buttons. Sparkline of effort log. Back-to-universe link.

content.subject: Canvas v1: react-konva with threads, orbs, pan/zoom, semantic zoom
content.description: Universe page at /petboard/. react-konva Stage with pan (drag empty space) and wheel zoom (centered on cursor). Render project threads as colored glowing lines from created_at to now. Feature orbs at created_at + lines to completed_at where applicable. Status-based orb appearance (open ring backlog, solid done, pulsing in_progress, faint dropped). Vertical "now" line. Semantic zoom: zoomed-out shows just threads; mid zoom shows project labels + progress; zoomed-in shows feature cards.

content.subject: Canvas v2: drag-to-reposition, filters, time window, polish
content.description: Drag project label vertically to update canvas_y (PATCH on drop). Priority filter chips and status filter chips in top-left toolbar. Time-window slider at the top of the canvas that fades features outside the window to near-zero opacity (so completed features can stay "forever" but hide on demand). Top-right toolbar with zoom buttons + fit-all. Click a feature orb → side drawer with details. Click project label → navigate to /p/:slug.

1	// Package service is the internal business layer that both the REST
2	// handlers and (later) the MCP tools delegate to. Keeping it separate
3	// from transport means there's only one place that knows how a project
4	// gets created, how a status transition updates timestamps, etc.
5	package service
6	
7	import "errors"
8	
9	// Priority is the canonical set of project priority levels.
10	type Priority string
11	
12	const (
13		PriorityHigh   Priority = "high"
14		PriorityMedium Priority = "medium"
15		PriorityLow    Priority = "low"
16	)
17	
18	// Status is the canonical set of feature statuses. Transitions between
19	// them auto-set the matching timestamp on the feature row.
20	type Status string
21	
22	const (
23		StatusBacklog    Status = "backlog"
24		StatusInProgress Status = "in_progress"
25		StatusDone       Status = "done"
26		StatusDropped    Status = "dropped"
27	)
28	
29	// Project is the petboard row with computed aggregates the UI wants on
30	// the universe view.
31	type Project struct {
32		ID          int64    `json:"id"`
33		Slug        string   `json:"slug"`
34		Name        string   `json:"name"`
35		Problem     string   `json:"problem"`
36		Description *string  `json:"description,omitempty"`
37		Priority    Priority `json:"priority"`
38		Color       string   `json:"color"`
39		CreatedAt   int64    `json:"created_at"`
40		ArchivedAt  *int64   `json:"archived_at,omitempty"`
41		CanvasX     *float64 `json:"canvas_x,omitempty"`
42		CanvasY     *float64 `json:"canvas_y,omitempty"`
43	
44		// Aggregates — only populated on list/get responses.
45		FeatureCounts map[Status]int `json:"feature_counts,omitempty"`
46		TotalMinutes  int64          `json:"total_minutes"`
47	}
48	
49	// Feature is a single backlog item under a project.
50	type Feature struct {
51		ID          int64   `json:"id"`
52		ProjectID   int64   `json:"project_id"`
53		Title       string  `json:"title"`
54		Description *string `json:"description,omitempty"`
55		Status      Status  `json:"status"`
56		CreatedAt   int64   `json:"created_at"`
57		StartedAt   *int64  `json:"started_at,omitempty"`
58		CompletedAt *int64  `json:"completed_at,omitempty"`
59		DroppedAt   *int64  `json:"dropped_at,omitempty"`
60	}
61	
62	// EffortLog records a chunk of work against a project (optionally tied
63	// to a specific feature).
64	type EffortLog struct {
65		ID        int64   `json:"id"`
66		ProjectID int64   `json:"project_id"`
67		FeatureID *int64  `json:"feature_id,omitempty"`
68		Minutes   int64   `json:"minutes"`
69		Note      *string `json:"note,omitempty"`
70		LoggedAt  int64   `json:"logged_at"`
71	}
72	
73	// ProjectDetail bundles a project with its features and recent effort
74	// log — what GET /api/projects/:slug returns and what MCP get_project
75	// returns.
76	type ProjectDetail struct {
77		Project
78		Features []Feature   `json:"features"`
79		Effort   []EffortLog `json:"effort"`
80	}
81	
82	// CreateProjectInput captures the fields required to create a project.
83	// `Problem` is mandatory — validation is enforced in the service layer.
84	type CreateProjectInput struct {
85		Name        string
86		Problem     string
87		Priority    Priority
88		Description *string
89		Color       *string // optional override; derived from slug if empty
90	}
91	
92	// UpdateProjectInput holds the nullable fields accepted by PATCH. Only
93	// non-nil fields are applied.
94	type UpdateProjectInput struct {
95		Name        *string
96		Problem     *string
97		Description *string
98		Priority    *Priority
99		Color       *string
100		CanvasX     *float64
101		CanvasY     *float64
102		Archived    *bool
103	}
104	
105	// CreateFeatureInput captures the fields required to create a feature.
106	type CreateFeatureInput struct {
107		Title       string
108		Description *string
109	}
110	
111	// UpdateFeatureInput is what PATCH /api/features/:id accepts.
112	type UpdateFeatureInput struct {
113		Title       *string
114		Description *string
115		Status      *Status
116	}
117	
118	// LogEffortInput is the payload for POST .../effort.
119	type LogEffortInput struct {
120		Minutes   int64
121		Note      *string
122		FeatureID *int64
123	}
124	
125	// Sentinel errors for common failure modes so handlers can map them to
126	// the right HTTP status without string-matching.
127	var (
128		ErrNotFound         = errors.New("not found")
129		ErrInvalidInput     = errors.New("invalid input")
130		ErrDuplicateSlug    = errors.New("duplicate slug")
131		ErrInvalidTransition = errors.New("invalid status transition")
132	)
133	
134	// ValidPriority reports whether p is a known priority value.
135	func ValidPriority(p Priority) bool {
136		switch p {
137		case PriorityHigh, PriorityMedium, PriorityLow:
138			return true
139		}
140		return false
141	}
142	
143	// ValidStatus reports whether s is a known feature status.
144	func ValidStatus(s Status) bool {
145		switch s {
146		case StatusBacklog, StatusInProgress, StatusDone, StatusDropped:
147			return true
148		}
149		return false
150	}
151

content.subject: Probe Caddy forward_auth for X-Forwarded-Uri
content.description: Verify that Caddy's forward_auth directive automatically sets the X-Forwarded-Uri header on the subrequest to alive-server's /api/auth/verify, so alive-server can read the original request URI to make public-path decisions. Also discovered that alive-server did not preserve return URLs across the Google OAuth round-trip — fixed in task #2.

content.subject: SSE live updates from server to canvas
content.description: Server: emit SSE events on every mutation (project.created, project.updated, feature.created, feature.status_changed, effort.logged, etc.) on /petboard/api/events. Client: subscribe via EventSource, invalidate react-query caches on matching events so the canvas animates when Claude or the UI mutates state.

content.subject: Implement petboard OAuth 2.1 endpoints
content.description: Add migration 0002_oauth.sql (oauth_clients, oauth_auth_codes, oauth_access_tokens — all tokens as SHA-256 hashes). Implement /petboard/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server and /petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource metadata endpoints. POST /petboard/oauth/register (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration). GET /petboard/oauth/authorize (Caddy-authed, mints auth code with PKCE challenge). POST /petboard/oauth/token (PKCE verifier check, mints 30-day access token). Bearer middleware for /petboard/mcp.

content.subject: Implement MCP endpoint and tools
content.description: Streamable HTTP MCP transport at /petboard/mcp behind Bearer middleware. Expose tools: list_projects, get_project, create_project (problem required), update_project, add_feature, set_feature_status, update_feature, log_effort, search. All tools delegate to the service package — same code path the REST API uses.

content.subject: Write Claude Code skill and connect end-to-end
content.description: Create dotfiels/claude/skills/petboard.md with the trigger conditions, problem-statement writing guidance (with good/bad examples and the "when/who/because/today" template), session start/end effort logging policy, priority bumping policy, naming conventions for features. End-to-end test: start fresh Claude session, talk about a new pet project, verify it gets created via MCP and shows up on the canvas.

content.subject: Polish: errors, empty states, keyboard shortcuts
content.description: Consistent error rendering (toast + inline), empty states for the universe view and the project board, keyboard shortcuts (j/k to cycle project focus, space to fit-all, escape to close drawer), loading skeletons, dark-mode polish.

content.subject: Implement alive-server public-path registry (V2) and return-URL preservation
content.description: Two related changes to base-setup/alive-server/main.go: (A) Generic public-path registry that reads /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf into an in-memory prefix list at startup and on SIGHUP. handleAuthVerify consults the registry first using X-Forwarded-Uri and short-circuits to 200 OK for any registered prefix. (B) Return-URL preservation across the Google OAuth round-trip: handleAuthVerify passes the original URI as return_to=, the state store carries it through to handleOAuth2Callback, and the callback redirects to the captured URL (validated via isSafeRelativePath). All changes already in commit 3d347fa on origin/main.

content.subject: Scaffold petboard directory tree
content.description: Create the services/petboard/ layout per PLAN.md: server/ (go module, cmd, db, api, oauth, mcp, service, static), web/ (Vite + React + TS + Tailwind project), install-petboard.sh stub, uninstall-petboard.sh stub, petboard.caddy, README.md. Both go build and npm run build must succeed on the empty scaffold.

content.subject: Build petboard backend v1 (CRUD + seed loader)
content.description: SQLite migrations (0001_init.sql with projects+features+effort_logs, mandatory problem field). Projects CRUD with validation (non-empty trimmed problem). Features CRUD with auto-timestamp on status transitions. Effort logs append-only with aggregate update. Bootstrap seed runs on empty DB and plants petboard as the first project. List endpoint returns aggregates (feature_counts, total_minutes). Verified locally with curl on http://127.0.0.1:7690/petboard/...

content.subject: Install script, systemd unit, Caddy snippet
content.description: Complete install-petboard.sh: create petboard-svc nologin user, provision /var/lib/petboard, build web + server as agnostic-user with explicit PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:..., install binary, write systemd unit, install /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/petboard.conf, deploy petboard.caddy, systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP alive-server. Write uninstall-petboard.sh that keeps /var/lib/petboard. PATH fix landed after first VM deploy attempt failed with "go: command not found" because sudo -u runs a non-login shell.

content.subject: Frontend scaffold (fetch /api/projects, render list)
content.description: Add react-router with basename="/petboard" and two routes: / → Universe, /p/:slug → ProjectDetail. Add QueryClientProvider around the app. Build a tiny api/client.ts — a thin wrapper over fetch that prepends /petboard/api and throws on non-2xx responses. In Universe.tsx fetch /api/projects via useQuery and render a plain unordered list: name, priority pill, progress ring ("7/12"), total hours. Ugly is fine, no canvas yet. This is the smoke test that proves the frontend ↔ backend round-trips through the real auth gate on the VM.

content.subject: Build project detail page
content.description: Route /petboard/p/:slug. Header with name + priority + color + created date + total effort. Editable problem block as pull-quote (click to edit, blur to save, PATCH to API). Four-column board: Backlog / In Progress / Done / Dropped. Feature cards with title, dates, status chip. "Add feature" and "Log effort" buttons. Sparkline of effort log. Back-to-universe link.

content.subject: Canvas v1: react-konva with threads, orbs, pan/zoom, semantic zoom
content.description: Universe page at /petboard/. react-konva Stage with pan (drag empty space) and wheel zoom (centered on cursor). Render project threads as colored glowing lines from created_at to now. Feature orbs at created_at + lines to completed_at where applicable. Status-based orb appearance (open ring backlog, solid done, pulsing in_progress, faint dropped). Vertical "now" line. Semantic zoom: zoomed-out shows just threads; mid zoom shows project labels + progress; zoomed-in shows feature cards.

content.subject: Canvas v2: drag-to-reposition, filters, time window, polish
content.description: Drag project label vertically to update canvas_y (PATCH on drop). Priority filter chips and status filter chips in top-left toolbar. Time-window slider at the top of the canvas that fades features outside the window to near-zero opacity (so completed features can stay "forever" but hide on demand). Top-right toolbar with zoom buttons + fit-all. Click a feature orb → side drawer with details. Click project label → navigate to /p/:slug.

content.text: Now build the frontend and verify it works.

attachment.prompt: VERY important! for this session do not do any action that might shutdown the VM or crash the /terminal service!

content.subject: Probe Caddy forward_auth for X-Forwarded-Uri
content.description: Verify that Caddy's forward_auth directive automatically sets the X-Forwarded-Uri header on the subrequest to alive-server's /api/auth/verify, so alive-server can read the original request URI to make public-path decisions. Also discovered that alive-server did not preserve return URLs across the Google OAuth round-trip — fixed in task #2.

content.subject: SSE live updates from server to canvas
content.description: Server: emit SSE events on every mutation (project.created, project.updated, feature.created, feature.status_changed, effort.logged, etc.) on /petboard/api/events. Client: subscribe via EventSource, invalidate react-query caches on matching events so the canvas animates when Claude or the UI mutates state.

content.subject: Implement petboard OAuth 2.1 endpoints
content.description: Add migration 0002_oauth.sql (oauth_clients, oauth_auth_codes, oauth_access_tokens — all tokens as SHA-256 hashes). Implement /petboard/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server and /petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource metadata endpoints. POST /petboard/oauth/register (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration). GET /petboard/oauth/authorize (Caddy-authed, mints auth code with PKCE challenge). POST /petboard/oauth/token (PKCE verifier check, mints 30-day access token). Bearer middleware for /petboard/mcp.

content.subject: Implement MCP endpoint and tools
content.description: Streamable HTTP MCP transport at /petboard/mcp behind Bearer middleware. Expose tools: list_projects, get_project, create_project (problem required), update_project, add_feature, set_feature_status, update_feature, log_effort, search. All tools delegate to the service package — same code path the REST API uses.

content.subject: Write Claude Code skill and connect end-to-end
content.description: Create dotfiels/claude/skills/petboard.md with the trigger conditions, problem-statement writing guidance (with good/bad examples and the "when/who/because/today" template), session start/end effort logging policy, priority bumping policy, naming conventions for features. End-to-end test: start fresh Claude session, talk about a new pet project, verify it gets created via MCP and shows up on the canvas.

content.subject: Polish: errors, empty states, keyboard shortcuts
content.description: Consistent error rendering (toast + inline), empty states for the universe view and the project board, keyboard shortcuts (j/k to cycle project focus, space to fit-all, escape to close drawer), loading skeletons, dark-mode polish.

content.subject: Implement alive-server public-path registry (V2) and return-URL preservation
content.description: Two related changes to base-setup/alive-server/main.go: (A) Generic public-path registry that reads /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf into an in-memory prefix list at startup and on SIGHUP. handleAuthVerify consults the registry first using X-Forwarded-Uri and short-circuits to 200 OK for any registered prefix. (B) Return-URL preservation across the Google OAuth round-trip: handleAuthVerify passes the original URI as return_to=, the state store carries it through to handleOAuth2Callback, and the callback redirects to the captured URL (validated via isSafeRelativePath). All changes already in commit 3d347fa on origin/main.

content.subject: Scaffold petboard directory tree
content.description: Create the services/petboard/ layout per PLAN.md: server/ (go module, cmd, db, api, oauth, mcp, service, static), web/ (Vite + React + TS + Tailwind project), install-petboard.sh stub, uninstall-petboard.sh stub, petboard.caddy, README.md. Both go build and npm run build must succeed on the empty scaffold.

content.subject: Build petboard backend v1 (CRUD + seed loader)
content.description: SQLite migrations (0001_init.sql with projects+features+effort_logs, mandatory problem field). Projects CRUD with validation (non-empty trimmed problem). Features CRUD with auto-timestamp on status transitions. Effort logs append-only with aggregate update. Bootstrap seed runs on empty DB and plants petboard as the first project. List endpoint returns aggregates (feature_counts, total_minutes). Verified locally with curl on http://127.0.0.1:7690/petboard/...

content.subject: Install script, systemd unit, Caddy snippet
content.description: Complete install-petboard.sh: create petboard-svc nologin user, provision /var/lib/petboard, build web + server as agnostic-user with explicit PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:..., install binary, write systemd unit, install /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/petboard.conf, deploy petboard.caddy, systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP alive-server. Write uninstall-petboard.sh that keeps /var/lib/petboard. PATH fix landed after first VM deploy attempt failed with "go: command not found" because sudo -u runs a non-login shell.

content.subject: Frontend scaffold (fetch /api/projects, render list)
content.description: Add react-router with basename="/petboard" and two routes: / → Universe, /p/:slug → ProjectDetail. Add QueryClientProvider around the app. Build a tiny api/client.ts — a thin wrapper over fetch that prepends /petboard/api and throws on non-2xx responses. In Universe.tsx fetch /api/projects via useQuery and render a plain unordered list: name, priority pill, progress ring ("7/12"), total hours. Ugly is fine, no canvas yet. This is the smoke test that proves the frontend ↔ backend round-trips through the real auth gate on the VM.

content.subject: Build project detail page
content.description: Route /petboard/p/:slug. Header with name + priority + color + created date + total effort. Editable problem block as pull-quote (click to edit, blur to save, PATCH to API). Four-column board: Backlog / In Progress / Done / Dropped. Feature cards with title, dates, status chip. "Add feature" and "Log effort" buttons. Sparkline of effort log. Back-to-universe link.

content.subject: Canvas v1: react-konva with threads, orbs, pan/zoom, semantic zoom
content.description: Universe page at /petboard/. react-konva Stage with pan (drag empty space) and wheel zoom (centered on cursor). Render project threads as colored glowing lines from created_at to now. Feature orbs at created_at + lines to completed_at where applicable. Status-based orb appearance (open ring backlog, solid done, pulsing in_progress, faint dropped). Vertical "now" line. Semantic zoom: zoomed-out shows just threads; mid zoom shows project labels + progress; zoomed-in shows feature cards.

content.subject: Canvas v2: drag-to-reposition, filters, time window, polish
content.description: Drag project label vertically to update canvas_y (PATCH on drop). Priority filter chips and status filter chips in top-left toolbar. Time-window slider at the top of the canvas that fades features outside the window to near-zero opacity (so completed features can stay "forever" but hide on demand). Top-right toolbar with zoom buttons + fit-all. Click a feature orb → side drawer with details. Click project label → navigate to /p/:slug.

1	// Package api implements the REST HTTP surface of petboard. Every
2	// handler delegates business logic to the service package; this layer
3	// is only about decoding requests, mapping errors to HTTP status codes,
4	// and emitting JSON responses.
5	package api
6	
7	import (
8		"encoding/json"
9		"errors"
10		"log"
11		"net/http"
12		"strconv"
13		"strings"
14	
15		"petboard/service"
16	)
17	
18	// API bundles the dependencies every handler needs.
19	type API struct {
20		Svc *service.Service
21	}
22	
23	// Register attaches every petboard REST route to the given mux under
24	// the /api prefix. Caller is responsible for any outer path prefix
25	// (e.g. Caddy mapping /petboard/* to this server) — routes are
26	// registered with their final on-server paths.
27	func (a *API) Register(mux *http.ServeMux) {
28		// Projects
29		mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/projects", a.listProjects)
30		mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/projects", a.createProject)
31		mux.HandleFunc("GET /api/projects/{slug}", a.getProject)
32		mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /api/projects/{slug}", a.updateProject)
33		mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /api/projects/{slug}", a.deleteProject)
34	
35		// Nested under projects
36		mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/projects/{slug}/features", a.createFeature)
37		mux.HandleFunc("POST /api/projects/{slug}/effort", a.logEffort)
38	
39		// Features
40		mux.HandleFunc("PATCH /api/features/{id}", a.updateFeature)
41		mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /api/features/{id}", a.deleteFeature)
42	}
43	
44	// --- shared helpers ----------------------------------------------------
45	
46	// writeJSON serializes v as JSON with the given status. If encoding
47	// fails we log it but there's nothing useful to send to the client at
48	// that point — the headers are already on the wire.
49	func writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) {
50		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json; charset=utf-8")
51		w.WriteHeader(status)
52		if err := json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(v); err != nil {
53			log.Printf("api: encode response: %v", err)
54		}
55	}
56	
57	// writeError maps a service error to an appropriate HTTP status and a
58	// JSON body. Unknown errors become 500.
59	func writeError(w http.ResponseWriter, err error) {
60		switch {
61		case errors.Is(err, service.ErrNotFound):
62			writeJSON(w, http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
63		case errors.Is(err, service.ErrInvalidInput):
64			writeJSON(w, http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
65		case errors.Is(err, service.ErrDuplicateSlug):
66			writeJSON(w, http.StatusConflict, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
67		case errors.Is(err, service.ErrInvalidTransition):
68			writeJSON(w, http.StatusConflict, map[string]string{"error": err.Error()})
69		default:
70			log.Printf("api: unexpected error: %v", err)
71			writeJSON(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "internal error"})
72		}
73	}
74	
75	// decodeBody reads a JSON body into dst. Returns an error suitable for
76	// writeError (wrapped as ErrInvalidInput) on malformed JSON.
77	func decodeBody(r *http.Request, dst any) error {
78		dec := json.NewDecoder(r.Body)
79		dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
80		if err := dec.Decode(dst); err != nil {
81			return wrapInvalid("malformed JSON body: " + err.Error())
82		}
83		return nil
84	}
85	
86	// wrapInvalid builds an ErrInvalidInput-wrapped error with a message.
87	func wrapInvalid(msg string) error {
88		return &wrappedInvalid{msg: msg}
89	}
90	
91	type wrappedInvalid struct{ msg string }
92	
93	func (e *wrappedInvalid) Error() string { return "invalid input: " + e.msg }
94	func (e *wrappedInvalid) Unwrap() error { return service.ErrInvalidInput }
95	
96	// parseInt64 is a thin wrapper so path-value parsing reads cleanly at
97	// call sites.
98	func parseInt64(s string) (int64, error) {
99		return strconv.ParseInt(strings.TrimSpace(s), 10, 64)
100	}
101

1	package api
2	
3	import (
4		"net/http"
5	
6		"petboard/service"
7	)
8	
9	// logEffort handles POST /api/projects/{slug}/effort.
10	//
11	// The wire contract accepts minutes directly (matching the DB column)
12	// rather than hours. MCP tools convert hours → minutes at the boundary;
13	// REST callers send whatever they store.
14	func (a *API) logEffort(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
15		slug := r.PathValue("slug")
16		var body struct {
17			Minutes   int64   `json:"minutes"`
18			Note      *string `json:"note"`
19			FeatureID *int64  `json:"feature_id"`
20		}
21		if err := decodeBody(r, &body); err != nil {
22			writeError(w, err)
23			return
24		}
25		log, err := a.Svc.LogEffort(slug, service.LogEffortInput{
26			Minutes:   body.Minutes,
27			Note:      body.Note,
28			FeatureID: body.FeatureID,
29		})
30		if err != nil {
31			writeError(w, err)
32			return
33		}
34		writeJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, log)
35	}
36

content.subject: Probe Caddy forward_auth for X-Forwarded-Uri
content.description: Verify that Caddy's forward_auth directive automatically sets the X-Forwarded-Uri header on the subrequest to alive-server's /api/auth/verify, so alive-server can read the original request URI to make public-path decisions. Also discovered that alive-server did not preserve return URLs across the Google OAuth round-trip — fixed in task #2.

content.subject: SSE live updates from server to canvas
content.description: Server: emit SSE events on every mutation (project.created, project.updated, feature.created, feature.status_changed, effort.logged, etc.) on /petboard/api/events. Client: subscribe via EventSource, invalidate react-query caches on matching events so the canvas animates when Claude or the UI mutates state.

content.subject: Implement petboard OAuth 2.1 endpoints
content.description: Add migration 0002_oauth.sql (oauth_clients, oauth_auth_codes, oauth_access_tokens — all tokens as SHA-256 hashes). Implement /petboard/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server and /petboard/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource metadata endpoints. POST /petboard/oauth/register (RFC 7591 dynamic client registration). GET /petboard/oauth/authorize (Caddy-authed, mints auth code with PKCE challenge). POST /petboard/oauth/token (PKCE verifier check, mints 30-day access token). Bearer middleware for /petboard/mcp.

content.subject: Implement MCP endpoint and tools
content.description: Streamable HTTP MCP transport at /petboard/mcp behind Bearer middleware. Expose tools: list_projects, get_project, create_project (problem required), update_project, add_feature, set_feature_status, update_feature, log_effort, search. All tools delegate to the service package — same code path the REST API uses.

content.subject: Write Claude Code skill and connect end-to-end
content.description: Create dotfiels/claude/skills/petboard.md with the trigger conditions, problem-statement writing guidance (with good/bad examples and the "when/who/because/today" template), session start/end effort logging policy, priority bumping policy, naming conventions for features. End-to-end test: start fresh Claude session, talk about a new pet project, verify it gets created via MCP and shows up on the canvas.

content.subject: Polish: errors, empty states, keyboard shortcuts
content.description: Consistent error rendering (toast + inline), empty states for the universe view and the project board, keyboard shortcuts (j/k to cycle project focus, space to fit-all, escape to close drawer), loading skeletons, dark-mode polish.

content.subject: Implement alive-server public-path registry (V2) and return-URL preservation
content.description: Two related changes to base-setup/alive-server/main.go: (A) Generic public-path registry that reads /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/*.conf into an in-memory prefix list at startup and on SIGHUP. handleAuthVerify consults the registry first using X-Forwarded-Uri and short-circuits to 200 OK for any registered prefix. (B) Return-URL preservation across the Google OAuth round-trip: handleAuthVerify passes the original URI as return_to=, the state store carries it through to handleOAuth2Callback, and the callback redirects to the captured URL (validated via isSafeRelativePath). All changes already in commit 3d347fa on origin/main.

content.subject: Scaffold petboard directory tree
content.description: Create the services/petboard/ layout per PLAN.md: server/ (go module, cmd, db, api, oauth, mcp, service, static), web/ (Vite + React + TS + Tailwind project), install-petboard.sh stub, uninstall-petboard.sh stub, petboard.caddy, README.md. Both go build and npm run build must succeed on the empty scaffold.

content.subject: Build petboard backend v1 (CRUD + seed loader)
content.description: SQLite migrations (0001_init.sql with projects+features+effort_logs, mandatory problem field). Projects CRUD with validation (non-empty trimmed problem). Features CRUD with auto-timestamp on status transitions. Effort logs append-only with aggregate update. Bootstrap seed runs on empty DB and plants petboard as the first project. List endpoint returns aggregates (feature_counts, total_minutes). Verified locally with curl on http://127.0.0.1:7690/petboard/...

content.subject: Install script, systemd unit, Caddy snippet
content.description: Complete install-petboard.sh: create petboard-svc nologin user, provision /var/lib/petboard, build web + server as agnostic-user with explicit PATH=/usr/local/go/bin:..., install binary, write systemd unit, install /etc/attlas-public-paths.d/petboard.conf, deploy petboard.caddy, systemctl kill --signal=SIGHUP alive-server. Write uninstall-petboard.sh that keeps /var/lib/petboard. PATH fix landed after first VM deploy attempt failed with "go: command not found" because sudo -u runs a non-login shell.

content.subject: Frontend scaffold (fetch /api/projects, render list)
content.description: Add react-router with basename="/petboard" and two routes: / → Universe, /p/:slug → ProjectDetail. Add QueryClientProvider around the app. Build a tiny api/client.ts — a thin wrapper over fetch that prepends /petboard/api and throws on non-2xx responses. In Universe.tsx fetch /api/projects via useQuery and render a plain unordered list: name, priority pill, progress ring ("7/12"), total hours. Ugly is fine, no canvas yet. This is the smoke test that proves the frontend ↔ backend round-trips through the real auth gate on the VM.

content.subject: Build project detail page
content.description: Route /petboard/p/:slug. Header with name + priority + color + created date + total effort. Editable problem block as pull-quote (click to edit, blur to save, PATCH to API). Four-column board: Backlog / In Progress / Done / Dropped. Feature cards with title, dates, status chip. "Add feature" and "Log effort" buttons. Sparkline of effort log. Back-to-universe link.

content.subject: Canvas v1: react-konva with threads, orbs, pan/zoom, semantic zoom
content.description: Universe page at /petboard/. react-konva Stage with pan (drag empty space) and wheel zoom (centered on cursor). Render project threads as colored glowing lines from created_at to now. Feature orbs at created_at + lines to completed_at where applicable. Status-based orb appearance (open ring backlog, solid done, pulsing in_progress, faint dropped). Vertical "now" line. Semantic zoom: zoomed-out shows just threads; mid zoom shows project labels + progress; zoomed-in shows feature cards.

content.subject: Canvas v2: drag-to-reposition, filters, time window, polish
content.description: Drag project label vertically to update canvas_y (PATCH on drop). Priority filter chips and status filter chips in top-left toolbar. Time-window slider at the top of the canvas that fades features outside the window to near-zero opacity (so completed features can stay "forever" but hide on demand). Top-right toolbar with zoom buttons + fit-all. Click a feature orb → side drawer with details. Click project label → navigate to /p/:slug.

1	// petboard — personal project tracker service for attlas.
2	//
3	// One binary, three subcommands:
4	//
5	//   petboard serve    — run the HTTP server (REST + static SPA + later MCP)
6	//   petboard migrate  — apply SQLite schema migrations and exit
7	//   petboard version  — print the version string and exit
8	//
9	// Configuration is entirely via environment variables so the systemd
10	// unit can own it without a config file:
11	//
12	//   PETBOARD_DB          path to the sqlite database file
13	//   PETBOARD_PORT        TCP port to bind on 127.0.0.1
14	//   PETBOARD_STATIC_DIR  directory served at /petboard/ (vite dist/)
15	//
16	// See services/petboard/PLAN.md for the full design.
17	package main
18	
19	import (
20		"context"
21		"errors"
22		"flag"
23		"fmt"
24		"log"
25		"net/http"
26		"os"
27		"os/signal"
28		"path/filepath"
29		"strconv"
30		"strings"
31		"syscall"
32		"time"
33	
34		"petboard/api"
35		"petboard/db"
36		"petboard/service"
37	)
38	
39	const version = "0.1.0-dev"
40	
41	func main() {
42		log.SetFlags(log.LstdFlags | log.Lmicroseconds)
43	
44		if len(os.Args) < 2 {
45			fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "usage: petboard <serve|migrate|version>")
46			os.Exit(2)
47		}
48		switch os.Args[1] {
49		case "serve":
50			runServe(os.Args[2:])
51		case "migrate":
52			runMigrate(os.Args[2:])
53		case "version":
54			fmt.Println(version)
55		default:
56			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "petboard: unknown subcommand %q\n", os.Args[1])
57			os.Exit(2)
58		}
59	}
60	
61	// --- serve -------------------------------------------------------------
62	
63	func runServe(args []string) {
64		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("serve", flag.ExitOnError)
65		port := fs.Int("port", envInt("PETBOARD_PORT", 7690), "HTTP listen port (bound to 127.0.0.1)")
66		dbPath := fs.String("db", envString("PETBOARD_DB", "/var/lib/petboard/petboard.db"), "SQLite path")
67		staticDir := fs.String("static", envString("PETBOARD_STATIC_DIR", defaultStaticDir()), "React build directory (Vite dist/)")
68		_ = fs.Parse(args)
69	
70		conn, err := db.Open(*dbPath)
71		if err != nil {
72			log.Fatalf("petboard: open db: %v", err)
73		}
74		defer conn.Close()
75	
76		svc := service.New(conn)
77		apiHandler := &api.API{Svc: svc}
78	
79		// Inner mux: routes as the downstream sees them AFTER we strip the
80		// /petboard/ prefix.
81		inner := http.NewServeMux()
82		apiHandler.Register(inner)
83		inner.Handle("/", spaFileServer(*staticDir))
84	
85		// Outer mux: Caddy proxies /petboard/* to us. Strip the prefix so
86		// the inner mux matches plain /api/... and /.
87		outer := http.NewServeMux()
88		outer.Handle("/petboard/", http.StripPrefix("/petboard", inner))
89		outer.Handle("/petboard", http.RedirectHandler("/petboard/", http.StatusPermanentRedirect))
90	
91		addr := fmt.Sprintf("127.0.0.1:%d", *port)
92		srv := &http.Server{
93			Addr:         addr,
94			Handler:      loggingMiddleware(outer),
95			ReadTimeout:  15 * time.Second,
96			WriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,
97			IdleTimeout:  90 * time.Second,
98		}
99	
100		// Start the server in a goroutine so main can handle signals.
101		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
102		go func() {
103			log.Printf("petboard %s listening on http://%s (db=%s, static=%s)",
104				version, addr, *dbPath, *staticDir)
105			if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && !errors.Is(err, http.ErrServerClosed) {
106				errCh <- err
107			}
108			close(errCh)
109		}()
110	
111		// Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.
112		sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
113		signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
114		select {
115		case sig := <-sigCh:
116			log.Printf("petboard: received %s, shutting down", sig)
117			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
118			defer cancel()
119			if err := srv.Shutdown(ctx); err != nil {
120				log.Printf("petboard: shutdown error: %v", err)
121			}
122		case err := <-errCh:
123			if err != nil {
124				log.Fatalf("petboard: serve failed: %v", err)
125			}
126		}
127	}
128	
129	// --- migrate -----------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	func runMigrate(args []string) {
132		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("migrate", flag.ExitOnError)
133		dbPath := fs.String("db", envString("PETBOARD_DB", "/var/lib/petboard/petboard.db"), "SQLite path")
134		_ = fs.Parse(args)
135	
136		conn, err := db.Open(*dbPath)
137		if err != nil {
138			log.Fatalf("petboard: open db: %v", err)
139		}
140		conn.Close()
141		log.Printf("petboard: migrations applied")
142	}
143	
144	// --- static SPA server -------------------------------------------------
145	
146	// spaFileServer returns an http.Handler that serves static files from
147	// dir, falling back to dir/index.html for any path that isn't an
148	// existing file. That fallback is what lets client-side routes like
149	// /petboard/p/petboard deep-link correctly — the server treats the
150	// unknown path as a 200 with index.html, and React Router picks up.
151	//
152	// The MIME types for JS, CSS, and wasm are set explicitly because
153	// modernc.org/sqlite used to ship a wasm file and wait-staff browsers
154	// are picky; setting them unconditionally costs us nothing.
155	func spaFileServer(dir string) http.Handler {
156		// If the static directory doesn't exist yet (e.g. running the
157		// binary before the frontend has been built), surface a 503 with a
158		// helpful message instead of a confusing 404.
159		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
160			info, err := os.Stat(dir)
161			if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
162				http.Error(w,
163					"petboard: static directory not found at "+dir+
164						" — build the frontend (cd web && npm run build) first",
165					http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
166				return
167			}
168			// Clean and normalize the request path so ../ attempts are
169			// rejected by filepath.Join behavior plus the IsAbs check.
170			reqPath := r.URL.Path
171			if reqPath == "" || reqPath == "/" {
172				serveIndex(w, r, dir)
173				return
174			}
175			// Build the candidate file path rooted at dir. Reject any
176			// resolved path that escapes dir.
177			candidate := filepath.Join(dir, filepath.FromSlash(strings.TrimPrefix(reqPath, "/")))
178			rel, err := filepath.Rel(dir, candidate)
179			if err != nil || strings.HasPrefix(rel, "..") {
180				http.Error(w, "invalid path", http.StatusBadRequest)
181				return
182			}
183			if stat, err := os.Stat(candidate); err == nil && !stat.IsDir() {
184				setContentType(w, candidate)
185				http.ServeFile(w, r, candidate)
186				return
187			}
188			// Not a real file — fall through to index.html so the SPA router
189			// can take it.
190			serveIndex(w, r, dir)
191		})
192	}
193	
194	func serveIndex(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dir string) {
195		index := filepath.Join(dir, "index.html")
196		if _, err := os.Stat(index); err != nil {
197			http.Error(w, "petboard: index.html missing in "+dir, http.StatusServiceUnavailable)
198			return
199		}
200		// index.html must never be cached — we want React to always pick
201		// up the latest bundle references after a rebuild.
202		w.Header().Set("Cache-Control", "no-store")
203		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")
204		http.ServeFile(w, r, index)
205	}
206	
207	// setContentType applies a handful of MIME defaults for paths that
208	// http.ServeFile's built-in detection sometimes gets wrong on minimal
209	// systems.
210	func setContentType(w http.ResponseWriter, path string) {
211		switch {
212		case strings.HasSuffix(path, ".js") || strings.HasSuffix(path, ".mjs"):
213			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/javascript; charset=utf-8")
214		case strings.HasSuffix(path, ".css"):
215			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/css; charset=utf-8")
216		case strings.HasSuffix(path, ".svg"):
217			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "image/svg+xml")
218		case strings.HasSuffix(path, ".wasm"):
219			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/wasm")
220		}
221	}
222	
223	// --- middleware --------------------------------------------------------
224	
225	// loggingMiddleware writes a one-line access log per request. Keep it
226	// compact so the journalctl tail stays readable.
227	func loggingMiddleware(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
228		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
229			start := time.Now()
230			lw := &loggingResponseWriter{ResponseWriter: w, status: 200}
231			next.ServeHTTP(lw, r)
232			log.Printf("%s %s %d %s", r.Method, r.URL.Path, lw.status, time.Since(start))
233		})
234	}
235	
236	type loggingResponseWriter struct {
237		http.ResponseWriter
238		status int
239	}
240	
241	func (w *loggingResponseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {
242		w.status = code
243		w.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)
244	}
245	
246	// --- env helpers -------------------------------------------------------
247	
248	func envString(key, fallback string) string {
249		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
250			return v
251		}
252		return fallback
253	}
254	
255	func envInt(key string, fallback int) int {
256		v := os.Getenv(key)
257		if v == "" {
258			return fallback
259		}
260		n, err := strconv.Atoi(v)
261		if err != nil {
262			return fallback
263		}
264		return n
265	}
266	
267	// defaultStaticDir guesses where the vite build output lives when no
268	// explicit path is supplied. Preference order:
269	//
270	//  1. ../../web/dist relative to the binary — useful during local
271	//     development when running `go run ./cmd/petboard` from server/.
272	//  2. /usr/local/share/petboard/dist — the install script's layout.
273	//  3. ./web/dist — fallback for ad-hoc runs.
274	func defaultStaticDir() string {
275		if exe, err := os.Executable(); err == nil {
276			candidate := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(exe), "..", "..", "web", "dist")
277			if stat, err := os.Stat(candidate); err == nil && stat.IsDir() {
278				return candidate
279			}
280		}
281		if stat, err := os.Stat("/usr/local/share/petboard/dist"); err == nil && stat.IsDir() {
282			return "/usr/local/share/petboard/dist"
283		}
284		return "./web/dist"
285	}

-- 0002_oauth: tables for the MCP OAuth 2.1 flow.
--
-- Three tables:
--
--  oauth_clients         — RFC 7591 dynamic client registrations
--  oauth_auth_codes      — short-lived authorization codes (PKCE)
--  oauth_access_tokens   — long-lived bearer tokens (30 days)
--
-- We never store raw tokens or codes — only their SHA-256 hashes — so a
-- DB leak doesn't immediately compromise sessions. The plain values
-- live only in HTTP responses to the legitimate caller.

CREATE TABLE oauth_clients (
    id            TEXT PRIMARY KEY,             -- client_id, opaque
    name          TEXT NOT NULL,
    redirect_uris TEXT NOT NULL,                -- JSON array of strings
    created_at    INTEGER NOT NULL              -- unix seconds
);

CREATE TABLE oauth_auth_codes (
    code_hash             TEXT PRIMARY KEY,      -- sha256(code)
    client_id             TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES oauth_clients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    code_challenge        TEXT NOT NULL,
    code_challenge_method TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'S256',
    redirect_uri          TEXT NOT NULL,
    scope                 TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    user_email            TEXT NOT NULL,         -- captured from session at /authorize time
    expires_at            INTEGER NOT NULL,      -- unix seconds, ~10 minutes from issuance
    used                  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE INDEX idx_oauth_auth_codes_expires ON oauth_auth_codes(expires_at);

CREATE TABLE oauth_access_tokens (
    token_hash    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,              -- sha256(token)
    client_id     TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES oauth_clients(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    scope         TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    user_email    TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    expires_at    INTEGER NOT NULL,              -- unix seconds, ~30 days
    last_used_at  INTEGER
);

CREATE INDEX idx_oauth_access_tokens_expires ON oauth_access_tokens(expires_at);
